RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE
Not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. Sec 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date of February 22, 2021 is vacated. The new trial date is November 23, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. Sec 36(f) is December 4, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is November 19, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 6, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is November 20, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 4, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason Kort of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant and moving party RIC-WIL, Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and opposing party George Alexander Oakes III

RULING: Defendant Ric-Wil's Motion For Summary Judgment
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ric-Wil's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing the Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Plaintiff testified that he could not recall where he saw the name Ric-Wil on the piping, not that he did not see Ric-Wil written on any of the piping. (Kujawski Declaration, Ex. D at 368.) Defendant also failed to attach its special interrogatories with its moving papers. Kujawski Declaration, Ex. B is captioned Defendant Ric-Wil, Inc.'s Special Interrogatories to Plaintiff George Alexander Oakes III, Set One and contains a list of Definitions, but no questions. The Court therefore cannot make any determination on the sufficiency of the discovery responses. The Court does not address Plaintiff's exposure on or after December 5, 1980 in light of Plaintiff's December 26, 2019 dismissal of those allegations. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293 sfdepo@veritext.com (Tel: 415-274-9977) (503/CML)
